text: Undergraduate advisors: Alexis Vasquez (ECE 263, 520.621.6171, alexisvasquez@arizona.edu) and Selim Orbay (ECE 261, 520.621.2434, sao@arizona.edu).
Graduate Academic Advisor: Jorge Camarillo (ECE 266). Phone 520.621.0575. Email jorgecamarillo@arizona.edu.
Undergraduate admissions: apply via UA portal; check deadlines and requirements for first-year and transfer students.
Transfer applicants submit official college transcripts, check course transferability, and watch term deadlines.
Graduate applications use GradApp; typical docs: SOP, CV, transcripts, 3 recommendation letters; English proficiency for international students.
Graduate deadlines are program-specific with priority dates; see Graduate College deadlines listing.
International undergraduates: English proficiency and financial documentation required.
International graduate applicants: English proficiency and financial documents required.

BS Software Engineering - Required Coursework: Includes core Software Engineering courses, math/science foundations, general education, and a senior capstone. Course availability varies by term; always check the current schedule and catalog for offerings.

BS Software Engineering - Prerequisites: Many upper-division SFWE courses require prior CS/SE foundations and math prerequisites. Prerequisites are enforced at registration; verify them in the UA catalog and class schedule.

BS Software Engineering - 4-Year Planning (Arizona Online): follow seven-and-a-half-week terms and adjusted course sequencing. Meet advising to map courses to upcoming online offerings and balance workload per term.

BS Software Engineering - 4-Year Planning (Main Campus/Distance): Typical eight-semester guides balance CS/SE foundations, math/science, and electives. Plans shift with math placement, transfer credit, and catalog year; advising can tailor yours.
BS Software Engineering - Technical Electives: must be pre-approved and may vary by catalog year. Ask advising for the current approved list and how it fits your plan.

BS Software Engineering - Transfer Credit: Applicability depends on the official UA evaluation; submit transcripts and review equivalencies in UA tools. Process: send official transcripts, confirm course matches, and partner with SFWE advising to place credits in your plan.

BS Software Engineering - Course Registration: Register during your assigned window; clear holds and meet prerequisites first. If you hit errors (requisites/permits/waitlists), contact SFWE advising for options.

BS Software Engineering - Major Admission: University admission is through Undergraduate Admissions; program/major placement follows UA policies. For change-of-major or internal transfer to SFWE, meet with advising to review criteria and timing.

MS in Software Engineering — Admission Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Software Engineering, Computer Science, or related field. Minimum GPA of 3.0. GRE optional. Statement of purpose and two recommendation letters. TOEFL/IELTS required for international applicants.

MS in Software Engineering — Course Prerequisites: Programming in C++, Java, or Python. Data structures and algorithms. Software design principles. Computer systems concepts. Foundation courses may be required for non-CS backgrounds.

MS in Software Engineering — Required Coursework: SWENG 501 – Software Engineering Principles, SWENG 505 – Advanced Software Design, SWENG 510 – Software Project Management, SWENG 520 – Software Verification & Validation, SWENG 530 – Software Architecture & Design Patterns, and SWENG 540 – Secure Software Development.
MS in Software Engineering — Technical Electives: Cloud Computing, Software Security, Agile Software Development, Machine Learning for Engineers, Data Visualization, Embedded Systems
MS in Software Engineering — Specialization Tracks: Embedded & Cyber-Physical Systems, AI & Machine Learning Systems, Cloud & DevOps Engineering, Secure Software Systems, and Data-Centric Systems
MS in Software Engineering — Thesis vs Non-Thesis Options: Thesis Track: 6 units research and defense. Non-Thesis Track: coursework/capstone. Total 30 graduate units.
social media platforms — roles: Frontend Engineer, Backend Engineer, Mobile App Developer, Data Engineer. Salary: USD 1500 – 3500 / month.
cloud computing — roles: Cloud Solutions Architect, DevOps Engineer, Cloud Security Specialist. Salary: USD 1800 – 4500 / month.
embedded systems — roles: Firmware Engineer, IoT Developer, Systems Programmer. Salary: USD 1200 – 3000 / month.
artificial intelligence — roles: ML Engineer, AI Research Scientist, AI Product Developer. Salary: USD 2000 – 5000 / month.
automation — roles: Automation Engineer, RPA Developer, Test Automation Engineer. Salary: USD 1300 – 3200 / month.
cybersecurity — roles: Security Engineer, Penetration Tester, Cybersecurity Analyst. Salary: USD 1800 – 4500 / month.
machine learning — roles: ML Engineer, Data Scientist, NLP Engineer. Salary: USD 2000 – 4800 / month.
full stack development — roles: Full Stack Engineer, Software Developer, Web Applications Engineer. Salary: USD 1400 – 3800 / month.
PhD - admission requirements: Cumulative GPA of 3, Transcripts, 3 recommendation letters, and English proficiency (if applicable)

PhD - required course work: is defined by the PhD major and your advisory committee but it may consist of 36 credits of major coursework, 9 to 15 credits of minor coursework, and 18 dissertation credits.

PhD - potential minor options: The University of Arizona offers a variety of potential minor options for PhD students. Each minor is a minimum of 9 units and must be approved by the student's major professor. Students are encouraged to consult with their faculty advisors to explore the full range of minor options available.
PhD - required prerequisites: Complete a minimum of 36 units in the major subject and 9 units in the minor subject, Dissertation, maintain a cumulative GPA of 3.0

PhD - technical electives: Technical electives are selected with your advisor to support your research focus (e.g., advanced SE, ML/AI, systems,embedded, verification, cybersecurity). Confirm eligible course lists with your advisor.

PhD - research & advisors: The University of Arizona offers a diverse range of PhD research focus areas, reflecting its commitment to interdisciplinary collaboration and innovation. Scan recent faculty publications and center pages, then email potential advisors with a concise research fit to get more information.

PhD - qualifying exam: A qualifying examination or diagnostic evaluation may be required to demonstrate acceptability to pursue a doctorate as well as to determine areas of study where further course work is necessary.

PhD - dissertation: The structure of the dissertation varies depending upon the discipline and the program offering the degree. See the program's handbook for the details of how your program structures their dissertation requirement. A common measure of quality of the dissertation is whether it is publishable in disciplinarily-recognized publication venues.

PhD - funding: The University of Arizona offers a variety of funding opportunities for PhD students, including scholarships, fellowships, awards for dissertation research and travel, and more. These opportunities are designed to support graduate students in their research and academic pursuits.
BS application (general steps): 1) Submit the UA undergraduate application. 2) Provide high school transcripts. 3) ACT/SAT if available (optional for many cases). 4) Pay the fee
BS deadlines: Undergraduate deadlines vary by term (Fall/Spring) and residency.
BS documents: Typical BS documents: transcripts; optional test scores; any program forms UA requests during the app.
BS eligibility: High school diploma or equivalent (GED), Official transcripts, Program-specific expectations (e.g., GPA and prerequisites vary by major), Test scores (ACT/SAT) optional in many cases
BS dual-degree: Dual-degree options are mostly at the graduate level. BS students usually combine minors/certificates.
Graduate Eligibility is program-specific; generally a relevant accredited degree and solid academic background are expected.
Graduate documents: Transcripts, Statement of purpose, CV / résumé, Three recommendation letters, and English proficiency for international students
Graduate dual-degree: UA offers some dual/combined graduate programs (e.g., MS + MBA) but availability is program-specific.
